prompt: Two teams now want the nightly ops tables in machine-readable form: finance pulls them into Sheets (CSV) and the dashboard ingests JSON. Add to_csv() and to_json() methods to Table in reporttable.py. I have strong opinions about the escaping because we got burned by a hand-rolled exporter before: CSV must be RFC-4180 shaped — CRLF row endings including after the last row, header row first, a field quoted only when it contains a comma, quote, newline or carriage return, embedded quotes doubled, never gratuitous quoting (a space is not a reason to quote). Cell values get stringified for CSV; None becomes an empty field. to_json() returns a compact JSON array of row objects with keys in declared column order, and it must NOT stringify: ints stay numbers, explicit None becomes null, and non-ASCII text stays literal (no \uXXXX soup) — while cells that were simply missing at add_row time stay the empty string they've always normalized to. Empty table: CSV is just the header line, JSON is []. Exports are read-only — render() output afterward must be byte-identical to before, and every current behavior (alignment, validation, rows() copies) is frozen. Acceptance: python3 test_reporttable.py, and the test file itself is not to be touched.
